aduz9fn.title: Into the Abyss: Earth's Last Great Wilderness
aduz9fn.body: More than half our planet is covered by deep ocean. We've only seen a tiny bit of it — but every time we go down, we find creatures nobody knew existed. Now companies want to dig up the ocean floor. What we find might change what we choose to protect.

ojb605w.title: Sylvia Earle — 'Her Deepness'
ojb605w.body: She's spent over 7,000 hours underwater. She says: "No ocean, no life." She means it.

f3wmmk0.title: Deep Ocean — 3 questions
f3wmmk0.body:
1 · How much of the ocean have we explored?
About 5% · About half · Almost all

2 · What is Earth's deepest place called?
Challenger Deep · The Big Hole · Ocean Bottom

3 · How many deep-sea creatures make their own light?
About 1 in 10 · About 3 in 4 · Almost none

rrdnkqh.title: 20,000 Leagues Under the Sea
rrdnkqh.body: Jules Verne wrote this adventure story in 1870 — before submarines even existed. It's still one of the greatest ocean stories ever.

48bcse3.title: Should we mine the ocean floor?
48bcse3.body: The ocean floor has metals used to make phones and electric cars. But we don't know what lives there yet. Should we dig it up?

88o5166.title: One year — you choose
88o5166.body:
Live in an underwater station 500m down, no sunlight
— or —
Live on the Space Station, far from Earth

t6ezhxo.title: Why does the deep sea glow?
t6ezhxo.body: Three out of four deep-sea creatures make their own light. There's no sun down there, so animals evolved to glow instead.

k1p4g3j.title: What if someone lived down there?
k1p4g3j.body: Imagine creatures at the bottom of the ocean watching our rubbish drift down for hundreds of years. Would we owe them an apology?

h16qorg.title: 23 Jan 1960
h16qorg.body: Two people went deeper than anyone ever had — to the very bottom of the ocean. They found a fish living there. Life can survive anywhere.

yecwf32.title: The Mariana Trench
yecwf32.body: The deepest place on Earth — 11 km down. Mount Everest would fit inside with 2 km to spare. The pressure would crush a car.

z9wswia.title: Abyssal  /əˈbɪs.əl/
z9wswia.body: Means "so deep it seems bottomless." From an old Greek word.

7u91gy3.title: We know more about Mars than our ocean
7u91gy3.body: We've mapped nearly all of Mars. But 80% of Earth's ocean floor is still a mystery.

48t59tz.title: "Nothing lives in the deep sea" — Myth
48t59tz.body: Wrong! Whole ecosystems live there — with no sunlight at all. They use heat from the Earth instead.

inm067g.title: Tonight: go deeper
inm067g.body: Find one deep-sea creature that nobody at your dinner table has heard of. Tell them about it.

b9akthy.title: Design a deep-sea creature
b9akthy.body: No light. Ice cold. Crushing pressure. What does your creature look like? What does it eat?

aduz9fn.title: Into the Abyss: Earth's Last Great Wilderness
aduz9fn.body: More than half our planet sits under deep, crushing, lightless ocean. We've explored less than 25% of it — yet every descent finds species science has never seen. Mining companies now want to extract minerals from the seabed. What we discover may determine what we decide to protect.

ojb605w.title: Sylvia Earle — 'Her Deepness'
ojb605w.body: 7,000+ hours underwater. 100+ expeditions. First female chief scientist of NOAA. Her mission: protect at least 30% of the ocean by 2030.

f3wmmk0.title: Deep Ocean — 3 questions
f3wmmk0.body:
1 · What % of the ocean has been explored?
~5% · ~25% · ~50% · Nearly all

2 · Name of Earth's deepest point?
Challenger Deep · Titan's Pit · Hadal Trench · The Abyss

3 · What fraction of deep-sea creatures produce light?
~10% · ~40% · ~76% · Almost none

rrdnkqh.title: The Deep + 20,000 Leagues Under the Sea
rrdnkqh.body: 20,000 Leagues (Jules Verne, 1870) — the original submarine adventure. Then try The Deep (Rivers Solomon) — a haunting modern take on who might already live below.

48bcse3.title: Should we mine the ocean floor?
48bcse3.body: The seabed holds the rare minerals inside your phone and EV battery. But we barely know what lives there. Who decides — and who benefits?

88o5166.title: One year — you choose
88o5166.body:
Deep-sea research station at 500m — no sunlight, total darkness, bioluminescent neighbours
— or —
International Space Station — weightless, Earth views, 400km from home

t6ezhxo.title: Why the deep sea glows
t6ezhxo.body: 76% of deep-sea creatures produce their own light. No sunlight reaches below 200m, so evolution independently reinvented light from chemistry — dozens of times.

k1p4g3j.title: Do we owe them anything?
k1p4g3j.body: If complex creatures lived at the ocean floor and had been observing our waste descend for centuries — what moral obligations would we have toward them?

h16qorg.title: 23 Jan 1960
h16qorg.body: Piccard & Walsh reached the Mariana Trench floor in the bathyscaphe Trieste. They found a flatfish — proof that life exists at the very bottom of the world.

yecwf32.title: The Mariana Trench
yecwf32.body: Western Pacific. 11 km deep — enough to submerge Everest with 2 km to spare. Pressure 1,000× the surface. Home to bacteria, amphipods, and — increasingly — plastic.

z9wswia.title: Abyssal  /əˈbɪs.əl/
z9wswia.body: From Greek abyssos — bottomless. Relating to the ocean zone below 3,000m, where light never reaches.

7u91gy3.title: Mars is better mapped than our ocean
7u91gy3.body: We've charted more of Mars's surface than Earth's ocean floor. 80% of our oceans remain uncharted at high resolution.

48t59tz.title: "The floor is barren" — Myth
48t59tz.body: Hydrothermal vents support entire ecosystems with zero sunlight. More species may live below 200m than above it.

inm067g.title: Tonight: go deeper
inm067g.body: Find a deep-sea creature nobody at your dinner table has heard of. Bring its scientific name and one surprising fact.

b9akthy.title: Design a creature at 10,000m
b9akthy.body: No light. Near-freezing. Crushing pressure. What adaptations does it need? What does it eat?

aduz9fn.title: Into the Abyss: Earth's Last Great Wilderness
aduz9fn.body: More than half our planet sits under 3,000 m of crushing, lightless ocean. We've explored less than 25% of it — yet every descent finds species science has never seen. Mining companies now want to drill it. What we discover may decide what we protect forever.

ojb605w.title: Sylvia Earle — 'Her Deepness'
ojb605w.body: 7,000+ hours underwater. 100+ expeditions. First female chief scientist of NOAA. "No ocean, no life. No blue, no green."

f3wmmk0.title: Deep Ocean — 3 questions
f3wmmk0.body:
1 · What % of the ocean has been explored?
~5% · ~25% · ~50% · Nearly all

2 · Name of Earth's deepest point?
Challenger Deep · Titan's Pit · Hadal Trench · The Abyss

3 · What fraction of deep-sea creatures glow?
~10% · ~40% · ~76% · Almost none

rrdnkqh.title: The Deep + 20,000 Leagues
rrdnkqh.body: Rivers Solomon's haunting deep-sea merpeople story and Jules Verne's original submarine adventure — two very different takes on what might live below.

48bcse3.title: Should we mine the ocean floor?
48bcse3.body: It holds the minerals inside your phone and EV battery. Should we drill before we know what lives there? Who gets to decide?

88o5166.title: One year — you choose
88o5166.body:
Deep-sea station at 500m, no sunlight
— or —
International Space Station, no ground

t6ezhxo.title: Why the deep sea glows
t6ezhxo.body: 76% of deep-sea creatures produce their own light. Evolution reinvented light from chemistry when the sun ran out.

k1p4g3j.title: Do we owe them anything?
k1p4g3j.body: If intelligent creatures lived at the ocean floor watching our waste drift down — what would we owe them?

h16qorg.title: 23 Jan 1960
h16qorg.body: Piccard & Walsh reached the Mariana Trench floor. They found a flatfish — life at the very bottom of the world.

yecwf32.title: The Mariana Trench
yecwf32.body: 11 km deep — enough to swallow Everest with 2 km to spare. Pressure 1,000x the surface.

7u91gy3.title: Mars is better mapped than our ocean
7u91gy3.body: 80% of our oceans remain uncharted at high resolution.

48t59tz.title: "The floor is barren" — Myth
48t59tz.body: Vents host entire food chains with zero sunlight.

inm067g.title: Tonight: go deeper
inm067g.body: Find a deep-sea creature nobody at dinner has heard of.

b9akthy.title: Design a 10,000m creature
b9akthy.body: No light. Crushing pressure. What does it eat?
